summary: Cleaned‑up audience tape of the legendary 11/8/70 Port Chester show – Morning Dew, Dark Star→The Main Ten→Dancin' and Baby Blue sparkle on a surprisingly clear recording.
review: This three‑set performance is hailed as one of the best from the fall‑1970 Capitol run: the acoustic set delivers a breathtaking “Morning Dew,” the electric second set erupts with a classic Dark Star → The Main Ten → Dancin' sequence, and rare gems like Baby Blue and a fiery Good Lovin’ round out the night.  The audience source, painstakingly cleaned by Ken & Judy Lee and later refined by the Port Chester Resurrection Society, retains a bit of hiss but is remarkably listenable for a 1970 AUD.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4‑5 stars, praising the performance (standout jams, rare songs) and noting only modest recording hiss.  The strong musical consensus outweighs minor sound‑quality complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Cleaned audience tape; reduced hiss and splices, but occasional hiss and mild distortion on very loud peaks.  Overall clarity is high for a 1970 AUD and captures crowd ambience.

standout_songs: Dark Star → The Main Ten → Dancin'
show_quality.setlist_flow: Acoustic opener sets a relaxed mood, followed by an eclectic electric set with daring segues and extended jams; the NRPS/ pedal‑steel cameo adds a unique flavor before closing on high energy.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring guitar tone on Morning Dew, scorching leads in Dark Star/Dancin', and inventive pedal‑steel work during the NRPS segment.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned briefly as part of the rhythm section that re‑enters the jam after the percussion interlude; generally noted for solid bass foundation throughout the long jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals highlighted on Dire Wolf and other acoustic numbers; his rhythm guitar provides steady support during the extended electric jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann and Mickey Hart receive kudos for driving the feedback‑laden sections of Dark Star and powering the high‑energy Dancin' jam.
